name: pagination
title: Pagination
description: Splits long result sets into pages: a nav landmark with an ordered list of page controls. Each control is a real link (href) or button (onClick); the current page carries aria-current="page" plus a visible border and heavier weight. Includes the paginationRange helper for ellipsis overflow.
useWhen: Search results, document lists, or data tables too long for one page
Server-rendered pages where each page has its own URL (pass href per control)
Client-side collections without per-page URLs (pass onClick — controls render as buttons)
avoidWhen: Content better consumed continuously on one page (prefer a single page or in-page navigation over paginating short content)
Rendering a disabled Previous/Next at the bounds — at the first page omit PaginationPrevious entirely, at the last omit PaginationNext (USWDS behavior; nothing inert is left in the tab order)
Sequential multi-step flows like forms (use a step indicator — steps are a process, not a result set)

keyboard: Tab moves through Previous, the page controls, and Next in document order; Shift+Tab moves backwards
Enter activates link controls; Enter or Space activates button controls (native element contracts)
The current page stays focusable (aria-current="page"); activating it is a no-op navigation
Ellipsis markers are aria-hidden spans — never focusable, never announced
At the first/last page Previous/Next are not rendered, so no disabled control sits in the tab order
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default — every control is min-h-11 and page numbers are additionally min-w-11
